programme.title: Arthur: The Last of Mary Moo Cow/Bitzi's Beau
programme.desc: When Mary Moo Cow is cancelled, D.W. is devastated. How can television--or life as she knows it--ever be the same? Buster hasn't yet met Bitzi's new boyfriend, but he fears the worst. What if he is a Mom-stealer?

programme.title: Arthur: Sue Ellen Gets Her Goose Cooked/Best of The Nest
programme.desc: Sue Ellen is the champion of the popular Internet game, Virtualgoose.com, but she’s become obsessed! And now the gang has moved onto Best of the Nest. Will any of his friends be willing to forgo their computers for a real, live adventure?

programme.title: Arthur: Francine's Pilfered Paper/Buster Gets Real
programme.desc: When Francine has a big paper over Thanksgiving, she copies from a website. There's nothing wrong with that, is there? Everyone loves the new Top Supermarket reality series on TV, except Arthur. Will it end his friendship with Buster?

programme.title: Arthur: Listen Up!/Arthur's New Old Vacation
programme.desc: When Francine's Bubbe passes away, Muffy tries to understand how she can help her friend. Then, when Arthur's family goes on the same vacation they take every year, he and Buster try to make it more exciting.

programme.title: Arthur: Happy Anniversary
programme.desc: Arthur's parents have been married for 10 years and it's time to celebrate! But the festivities are cut short when the van breaks down, stranding the whole family. Will the Reads miss their fancy dinner reservation?

programme.title: Arthur: Arthur and The Real Mr. Ratburn/Arthur's Spelling Trubble
programme.desc: It's the first day of third grade and Arthur's biggest fear has come true--his new teacher is the dreaded Mr. Ratburn. Then, it’s time for the all-school spellathon. Arthur is chosen to compete, and he finds facing Prunella and The Brain!

programme.title: Arthur: Night of the Tibble/Read and Flumberghast
programme.desc: The Tibble Twins are having their first sleepover! Will the Tibbles lock James in the dark basement? Then, a cupcake's been stolen! The primary suspect: Tommy Tibble. D.W. gathers a jury and demands that justice be served.

programme.title: Arthur: Room to Ride/The Frensky Family Fiasco
programme.desc: With Lance Armstrong’s support, Binky and his friends take to the streets to convince residents to vote for new bike safety lanes. Francine wants the Frenskys to be closer, but a weekly Family Night turns out to be harder than expected.

programme.title: Arthur: Arthur Goes To Camp/Buster Makes The Grade
programme.desc: Arthur and his friends go to summer camp where it's strictly boys vs. girls. Next, Buster Baxter is everyone's favorite jokester. Unfortunately, though, Buster's grades aren't as good as his jokes, and Buster may have to repeat third grade.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: BJ is surprised to learn that “The Land of Mother Goose” truly exists. Baby Bop catches Barney, BJ, and Riff wrapping a mysterious present in the caboose, she just can’t wait to see what it is.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: A Picture of Friendship
programme.desc: After a brief misunderstanding, BJ realizes he has many friends. He enjoys a day playing with Barney and the children.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: A-Counting We Will Go!
programme.desc: Barney and the kids help Baby Bop count to ten by playing games, and visiting a Make-Believe Market.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: Ryan tries to be just like Barney but soon learns that he is special when he's being himself! Barney explains to Baby Bop that the word "no" can help you avoid making mistakes or getting hurt.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: A Parade of Bikes
programme.desc: It's the day of the bike parade. But Nick has had a bike accident that leaves his bike in need of repair by Mr. Boyd. Barney helps Baby Bop and the children learn all about bicycles—and that they are a wonderful way to exercise and have fun.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: This Way In, This Way Out!
programme.desc: Angela is in the park to release her turtle after observing him with her classmates in school. Barney and the children discuss the meaning of in and out after observing the turtle going in and out of his shell.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Bunches of Boxes!
programme.desc: A day of imaginative play and fun for Barney and his young friends is kicked off by the arrival of a variety of boxes from the "Enjoy-Your-Toys Company." Surprisingly, every box is empty!

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Everybody's Got Feelings
programme.desc: Barney helps Baby Bop and BJ learn that everyone has feelings—happy, sad, angry—and sometimes all those feelings can be experienced in just one day.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Riffs Musical Zoo - Boats
programme.desc: Everyone is excited about an upcoming trip to the zoo. When the trip is postponed, Riff decides to create a musical zoo. He gathers instruments that sound like real animals. Everyone has a wonderful time at Riff's musical zoo.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Return of the Technodrome
programme.desc: To bring the Technodrome back to Earth, Shredder is instructed to take over the Niagara Falls hydroelectric station.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Beneath These Streets
programme.desc: While the Turtles, in disguise, are at a movie, Shredder interrupts a press conference to steal a new medi-laser.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Turtles on Trial
programme.desc: While the Turtles go on a talk show to prove their innocence to the world, Krang steals a robot and some Solarbenite from a military warehouse, with the intention of destroying the Turtles himself.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Attack of the 50-Foot Irma
programme.desc: While the Turtles are planning Irma's birthday party, Shredder steals an Exaboron Isotope from a meteorite crash. Krang creates an Exaboron-powered Enlarging Ray which causes six times normal growth, with the intention of using it on Shredder.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): The Maltese Hamster
programme.desc: Shredder gives futuristic laser weapons to mobster Tony Vivaldi and his underlings. Shredder wants to find the Maltese Hamster statue of him, which has inside it the formula for ultimate fuel for the Technodrome.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Sky Turtles
programme.desc: Krang creates a Gravity Altering Device which causes everyone in the city, including the Turtles, to start floating.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): The Old Switcheroo
programme.desc: Two huge electrodes at Cybertech Labs produce a startling effect on two long-time foes. The mind of the Turtles master Splinter winds up in the body of the evil Shredder - and vice versa, causing confusion among their associates.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Burne's Blues
programme.desc: Burne is out to prove to April that he can still dig in the trenches and find good stories after deriding her for failing to come up with anything good lately.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): The Fifth Turtle
programme.desc: On the occasion of a rare conjunction of the Earth, Moon, and Stars, Shredder steals some Capsidium Crystals.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Enter: The Rat King
programme.desc: April does a story on the rat problem the city is facing, and the Rat King kidnaps her. The Rat King is the leader of all the rats under his form of government known as the Ratocracy.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Turtles at the Earth's Core
programme.desc: The Turtles venture down to the Earth's core and discover a lost prehistoric world complete with dinosaurs.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: The Lemmings have unearthed a box filled with birthday fountains of light that spit forth giant bursts of sparks. They have a blast, but their new game is not really compatible with a bear's nap.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy's last jar of chocolate spread has been pinched by the Lemmings, who are savouring it in secret.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Wedged in his sofa to relieve his aching back, Grizzly baulks at getting up. He designs clever strategies to do everything from a distance, including confiscating the Lemming's radio-controlled toy robot transformer, which he will use as a butler.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: The vibrations created by Grizzy's snoring during his nap give the Lemmings an idea for a bouncing game. But when Grizzy changes positions and his snoring stops, the Lemmings must find other sources of vibrations.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy finds a birthday piñata in the ranger's living room. The idea of bursting open a papier-mâché statue with a bat as it says in the instructions pleases him.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Inside a mysterious cave, Grizzy and the Lemmings discover a magical Game of the Goose. The events displayed in the squares that the players land on come true in real life.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy is bothered by an object stuck in between the sofa cushions. It's a big fur lemming puppet glove and the Lemmings believe that the puppet is a Super Lemming who's stronger than Grizzy and they start to obey it.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzly discovers the leftovers of a Halloween party in the ranger's cabin, which was entirely redecorated for the occasion.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: When the last jar of chocolate spread they were fighting over sinks to the bottom of the river, Grizzy and the Lemmings have no choice but to dive in to recover it. But to their great surprise, they discover you can't breathe in water.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy finds a strange figurine with the power to switch the spirits of two living beings and finds himself imprisoned in the body of a caribou.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: While salmon fishing on the riverbank, Grizzly hauls in an unusual catch: an old hand mirror straight out of a fairytale; instead of showing the reflection of the person who holds it, the mirror shows an idealized vision of his surroundings.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: In a casing that fell from a circus caravan, the Lemmings find a magic stick that can enchant objects and make them come alive.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzly calls upon his fishing instincts to seduce the she-bear, but only succeeds in making a fool of himself. When he sees the Lemmings riding a magnificent wild salmon like rodeo cowboys, he tries to steal theirs.

programme.title: Total Drama Action
programme.desc: The latest challenge involves the boys competing against the girls at kung fu. They also have to brew tea at the top of a mountain for a kung fu master.

programme.title: Total Drama Action
programme.desc: The next challenge involves sci-fi movies, with contestants having to sleep in zero gravity and fly a jet simulator. Their biggest challenge is to see how long they can last in the G Force trainer, or `vomit comet'.

programme.title: Total Drama Action
programme.desc: Animal buddy movies are the theme of the latest challenge and the first thing the four remaining contestants have to do is train their animals to be like them.

programme.title: Total Drama Action

programme.title: Total Drama Action
programme.desc: More of the show-within-a-show, TDA Aftermath, including previously unseen clips.

programme.title: Pokemon: Indigo League
programme.desc: After being defeated by Sabrina, Ash realizes that he must catch a Ghost-type Pokémon to defeat the Saffron Gym Leader’s awesome Psychic-type Pokémon. He heads for Pokémon Tower in Lavender Town, where they are said to dwell.

programme.title: Pokemon: Indigo League
programme.desc: Leaving Pokémon Tower with Haunter in tow, Ash returns to Saffron City with only one goal in mind: to win a Gym Badge from the powerful Psychic-type Gym Leader, Sabrina!

programme.title: Pokemon: Indigo League
programme.desc: When Ash tries to catch a wild Monkey, it loses its cool and uses Thrash on the whole gang—even stealing Ash’s hat! Will Ash find a way to pacify this pugnacious Pokémon?

programme.title: Pokemon: Indigo League
programme.desc: After insulting Gym Leader Erika’s perfume at a department store, Ash must go to some outrageous lengths to even set foot in the Celadon Gym, let alone be deemed worthy to challenge her!

programme.title: Pokemon: Indigo League
programme.desc: Officer Jenny reveals that a mysterious sleep wave has been detected in the area for several days. Our heroes suspect this has something to do with why so many children have gone missing, and why the Pokémon at the Pokémon Center aren't getting better.

programme.title: Pokemon: Indigo League
programme.desc: At Brock’s persistent request, our heroes stop by a well-known place to Pokémon breeders for its Pokémon beauty salons.

programme.title: Pokemon: Indigo League
programme.desc: Outside Fuchsia City, our heroes come across Hitmonchan, a Fighting-type Pokémon. With little thought, Ash attempts to battle it, but he and Pikachu are quickly defeated. But who is the mysterious Trainer of this extraordinary Hitmonchan?

programme.title: Pokemon: Indigo League
programme.desc: When a blackout plunges Gringey City into darkness, Ash and friends go to investigate the nearby power plant, where they find Magnemite and a host of Grimer. Could they be responsible for the city’s power outage?

programme.title: Pokemon: Indigo League
programme.desc: Ash, Brock, Misty, and Pikachu are lost deep in the mountains. As they press on through a rough track that doesn’t show up on their map, they suddenly hear a large explosion!

programme.title: Pokemon: Indigo League
programme.desc: On his way to the next Gym Battle challenge, Ash, and his friends arrive at a massive mansion that seems to be a hidden ninjas' lair, appearing lost. Could this be the Fuchsia Gym?

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Laa-Laa walks up to say “Eh-oh”. Then she goes to find Po, Dipsy and Tinky Winky and brings them in to say “Eh-oh” too. The windmill turns and the Tubbies watch a group of children go paddling in the sea.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies want to go to sleep, but Tinky Winky finds Po already asleep in his bed. So he goes to look for somewhere else to sleep.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies line up to say 'Eh-oh' and then watch Matthew and Mark feed a baby calf. The Teletubbies play a game to see who can eat their Tubby Custard the slowest, but Po can't wait and eats her Tubby Custard very fast.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: It’s time for Tubby Toast. Po and Tinky Winky are so excited, they fall over. They join Dipsy and Laa-Laa to watch two children dig a big mud hole.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies giggle as they play their 'Through the Arch' game. The windmill stops them to watch a little girl and her dad go for a walk in the town.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies walk in sideways, then run off to watch a little boy play cricket with his dad. In Teletubbyland,Tinky Winky runs away with Laa-Laa’s ball. Laa-Laa chases after him, through the house, over the hills and behind the trees.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: A group of children sing some Christmas carols to the Teletubbies. Tinky Winky opens his present to find a beautiful star inside.

programme.title: Teletubbies

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies are very happy and have fun jumping all over Teletubbyland. The Teletubbies watch some children jumping. Tinky Winky makes up a dance.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Dipsy is out for a walk when a strong wind blows his hat away. The hat disappears into Home Hill, but Dipsy doesn't know where it has gone. Noo Noo blows the hat back out of Home Hill to Dipsy, who is very happy.

programme.title: The New Detectives: Blind Trust
programme.desc: A woman's body is found and investigators have little time and few clues to lead them to a killer who could strike again. Perpetrators try to conceal their crimes, yet savvy investigators can take the most obscure data and recreate a murder.

programme.desc: George Young worked his fingers to the bone to give his loved ones a better future: his wife, Tia, their three sons, even his friend Tim Lee. How was this generosity repaid? With deception, infidelity, and a plot to cash in on his death.

programme.title: Meet, Marry, Murder: Chacey Poynter
programme.desc: Bob Poynter was old enough to be Chacey’s father. And he was married. But those were minor obstacles to her. However, when their romance hit the skids, Chacey enlisted yet another of her lovers to help bring the marriage to a brutal end.

programme.title: Meet, Marry, Murder: David Clark
programme.desc: Melanie and David Clark’s marriage was the definition of toxic. He was controlling and angry, and she had affairs. They seemed unable to tear themselves apart - until one violent incident ended their marriage for good.

programme.title: Meet, Marry, Murder: Nat Fraser
programme.desc: Arlene McInnes and Nat Fraser had a whirlwind romance, marrying and having their first child within 18 months of meeting. But Nat soon revealed himself as a womanising, violent bully. When she rebelled against him, he hatched a plot to punish her.

programme.title: 72 Hours: Model Killer
programme.desc: A gorgeous young model is bludgeoned to death in her downtown apartment. The wounds to her head are so severe police initially presume she's been shot.

programme.title: 72 Hours: Dead End
programme.desc: A young couple hits the road, hitchhiking across North America. Free spirits keen to explore the world. When the young woman stops phoning home, her father is alarmed.

programme.title: 72 Hours: Bull's Eye
programme.desc: A Girl Guide leader is found dead on the floor of her bedroom. When an autopsy reveals a bullet lodged in her brain, detectives learn they’re investigating a murder.

programme.title: 72 Hours: Burning Obsession
programme.desc: In 1988, a promiscuous 22-year old boy from the Sikh community has one affair too many and ends up losing his life. Diligent detective work and DNA help police nail the killer who believed he had gotten away with murder.

programme.title: 72 Hours: Who Killed Santa?
programme.desc: In 2001, there are a series of murders of homeless people in Toronto. The latest killing is of a gentle man, well known in the area by residents who nicknamed him Santa Claus.

programme.title: 72 Hours: Angel of Death
programme.desc: An angel of mercy turns out to be an angel of death when the number of deaths in an Indiana Intensive Care Unit skyrockets. One astute nurse believes there must be more to this than just natural causes.

programme.title: 72 Hours: The Ghost
programme.desc: A cunning arsonist stays one step ahead of the police and terrifies a small farming community until behavioural profiling help detectives zero in on a very unlikely suspect.

programme.title: 72 Hours: Loch Ness Monster
programme.desc: In 1998, in a normally peaceful suburb, a pervert on a bicycle appears, performing sexual acts on himself and then attacking teenage girls. For reasons apparent to the residents, they nickname him after the Scottish Loch Ness monster.

programme.title: 72 Hours: Mad Bomber
programme.desc: In 1996 a serial bomber begins planting bombs in public places and terrorizes the Prince Edward Island community. The mystery person leaves a strange swastika as a calling card, eluding capture for eight years.

programme.title: 72 Hours: Double Cross
programme.desc: The investigation of the murder of a shady Montreal businessman takes detectives into the lurid underworld of drug dealers, con men, and crooked lawyers.

programme.desc: General Burkhalter comes to Stalag 13 determined to marry off his sister to Klink. Hogan has to find a planted German agent in the Underground.

programme.title: Hogan's Heroes

programme.desc: The Heroes need to get both a member of the French resistance and some bullet-proof vests out of Stalag 13.

programme.desc: The Germans have set up a money-counterfeiting operation in the camp,and Hogan is planning on destroying it.

programme.title: Hogan's Heroes

programme.title: Hogan's Heroes
programme.desc: Hogan takes Kinchloe to Paris to visit a friend of his who might help them get plans for the defense of Paris.

programme.desc: A disagreeable Russian pilot wants to escape,but refuses to go first to London. He wants to head east to Mother Russia.

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Rodeo Ton
programme.desc: 1970s El Toro mechanical bull used to train bull riders; silver WWII era banjo; a collection of 1800s handcrafted hunting duck decoys.

programme.desc: Ton & Allen arrive late to an auction. They uncover vintage bear traps and a bounty of street art. If the pieces belong to the artist, RISK, it could be their biggest score ever. If not, they'll be stuck with forged knockoffs.

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Allen's Ruff Day
programme.desc: Ton and Allen head to an auction in Ton's hometown and end up battling with a big-mouthed buyer. They discover a pair of tranquilizer dart guns and also end up fighting with a K9!

programme.desc: Scale RC Viper FanJet aircraft with a potent jet engine; 1920s Gundlach Korona large format field camera used for photography expeditions. Ton pilots a plane, but crashes and burns.

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Ton Voyage

programme.desc: 1870s French Jarre Dolne Knuckle Duster revolver-knife-brass knuckle combo, a rare original George Rodrigue "Blue Dog" screenprint and a 1980 autographed Raging Bull movie poster.

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Gangster Whiskey
programme.desc: Allen and Ton trek to an auction in Moreno Valley, CA, where two locker wins yield a vintage jukebox, a valuable baseball card and a Prohibition-era moonshine still.

programme.title: Teen Mom: Trial and Error

programme.title: Teen Mom: Senior Prom
programme.desc: Maci is heartbroken when Ryan announces he's taking her to court over custody of Bentley. Amber's fun birthday plans are ruined when Gary refuses to babysit.

programme.title: Teen Mom: Hello and Goodbye
programme.desc: Amber and Gary's wedding plans fall apart over an argument about how to raise Leah. Maci finally tells her parents she's moving two hours away with Bentley, but they're not happy.

programme.title: The Maury Show: Take the Test ... Did You Cheat?

programme.title: The Maury Show: Is My Man Sleeping With My Teen Nephew?

programme.title: The Maury Show: I Cheated With My Daughter's Girlfriend
programme.desc: These women are desperate to hear MAURY say the words “YOU ARE THE FATHER!” But these men want to hear him say “YOU ARE NOT THE FATHER!” But only MAURY’s got the results they’re looking for!

programme.title: The Maury Show: I'll Prove You Are Cheating on My Teen!
programme.desc: Instead of chocolates and dinners, these guests are being cheated on and dumped. Love may be blind, but lie detector tests and hidden cameras will reveal the truth!

programme.desc: When Harvard psychiatrist John Mack approached filmmaker Laurel Chiten asking her to make a movie about encounters with aliens, she thought he was crazy. But after meeting some of the so-called experiencers she was intruiged.

programme.title: Top 10: Secrets and Mysteries: SECRET CULTS AND ORDERS
programme.desc: Top 10 Secrets and Mysteries looks at secret cults and mysterious orders including Skull and Bones at Yale University, the Freemasons, Thule and Vril Societies, the Bullingdon Club, the Hermetic Order of the Golden Dawn

programme.title: Top 10: Secrets and Mysteries: SCIENCE AND MYSTERIES FOR 21. CENTURY
programme.desc: Top 10 Secrets and Mysteries looks at some of the mysteries of modern science including memory of water, reincarnation, the beginning of life, hypnosis, the secrets of Morphic fields, time travel, why we must sleep.

programme.title: Top 10: Secrets and Mysteries: PEOPLE WITHOUT IDENTITY
programme.desc: 10 Mysterious Figures: Individuals who emerged from obscurity, made their mark on history, then vanished without a trace. Their identities remain unconfirmed, shrouding them in mystery.

programme.title: Top 10: Secrets and Mysteries: NATURE’S MAGIC
programme.desc: Natural sites worldwide hold spiritual significance for civilizations, from ancient rituals to modern reverence, each crackling with unique energy and a hint of magic.

programme.title: Top 10: Secrets and Mysteries: MYSTERIOUS WORKS OF ART
programme.desc: Great art and its creators unveil mysteries more intricate than TV dramas. Amidst theft and intrigue, we persist in solving them. Yet, the truth often lies buried with those who created it, adding layers of intrigue to the art world.

programme.title: Top 10: Secrets and Mysteries: SECRETS AND MAGIC OF ALTERNATIVE MEDICINE
programme.desc: In this episode, we dive into untraditional healing and its mysteries, challenging conventional Western medicine. Explore alternative medical miracles that will captivate you.

programme.title: Top 10: Secrets and Mysteries: MYSTERIOUS AND UNSOLVED DISSAPEARANCES
programme.desc: Legally declared dead, bodies never found. Disappearances spark speculation, media attention, and unanswered questions. Where did they go?

programme.title: Museum Secrets: Inside the National Maritime Museum, London
programme.desc: From the musket shot that killed Admiral Nelson to an anti-piracy commando raid, discover the secrets inside the National Maritime Museum.

programme.title: Museum Secrets: Inside the Metropolitan Museum, New York
programme.desc: From the mystery of a king’s madness to a silver chalice some think is the Holy Grail, discover the secrets inside the Metropolitan Museum.

programme.title: Museum Secrets: Inside the National Museum of Anthropology, Mexico City
programme.desc: From ancient athletes who kick a severed head to a peasant dress that brings down an empire, discover the secrets inside the National Museum of Anthropology.

programme.title: Museum Secrets: Inside the Bardo Museum, Tunisia
programme.desc: From the real lives of gladiators to the connection between Star Wars and Tunisia, discover the secrets inside the Bardo Museum.

programme.title: Museum Secrets: Inside the State Historical Museum, Moscow
programme.desc: From the duel that killed a genius to Lenin’s Rolls-Royce, discover the secrets inside the State Historical Museum.

programme.title: Museum Secrets: Inside the Chateau of Versailles, Paris
programme.desc: From the schemes of Marie Antoinette to the spectrum of a star that reveals the origin of human life, discover the secrets inside the Chateau of Versailles.

programme.title: Angry Planet: Antarctica
programme.desc: From "Fin Del Mundo," George sails on a Russian icebreaker to Antarctica. After braving stormy Drake passage, he explores volcanic Deception Island, camps in Antarctic snow and paddles a kayak through icebergs calved off melting glaciers.

programme.title: Angry Planet: Arctic Winter
programme.desc: George links up with two experienced polar adventurers to explore frigid Canadian Arctic winter. The team uses skis to cross the fractured sea ice of Frobisher Bay, then dog teams to head North through the spectacular Auyuittuq Mountains.

programme.title: Angry Planet: Polynesian Volcanoes
programme.desc: In New Zealand, George learns that an undersea volcano 1000 miles North in Tonga is creating a new island. After nautical mishaps, clad in nothing but underwear and a life jacket, George becomes one of the first explorers on this new land.

programme.title: Angry Planet: Highway to Hail
programme.desc: George and his team head into "bears' cage", looking to get beat up by the biggest, baddest hailstones in Texas and Oklahoma. Their storm chasing vehicles don't look too pretty after coming through these massive storms.

programme.title: Angry Planet: Defying Gravity
programme.desc: George experiences extremes of aviation and space training aboard Czech-built Albatross fighter jet, inside Zero-G plane, simulating zero gravity by diving 30,000 feet in giant parabolas and in spinning centrifuges producing 6 G's of force.

programme.title: Angry Planet: Under Pressure
programme.desc: After submarine exploration of Grand Cayman Island, George dives with Canadian Armed Forces' experimental deep pressure unit. With explorers Chris Harvey-Clark and Phil Nuytten he investigates newly discovered "Black Smoker" deep sea vents.

programme.title: Angry Planet: Crystal Cave
programme.desc: In 2000 in Chihuahua, Mexico, miners found a cave filled with giant crystals. Temperatures reach 120 degrees F and humidity nearly 100%. George leads an Explorers Club Flag Expedition into this extreme environment.

programme.title: Naked Science: Jupiter
programme.desc: By far the largest planet, 1300 times the size of Earth, it’s a world so enormous it could swallow every planet and moon in the solar system and still have room to spare.

programme.title: Naked Science: Diamonds
programme.desc: Naked Science follows the story of a diamond from the creation of carbon inside the heart of a dying sun, through to the cutting of a diamond mine in South Africa.

programme.title: The New Frontier: GIANTS IN THE DARK
programme.desc: Asteroids, the debris from the early solar system hold tantalizing clues to planetary formation. The spacecraft Psyche readies to study a unique metallic asteroid.

programme.title: The New Frontier: Stars Above
programme.desc: Stars are a bit like human beings. They can be warm or cold, they come in all kinds of shapes and sizes.

programme.title: The New Frontier: Mars: Adapt Or Leave
programme.desc: As the latest successes and failures come and go Mars continues to give up her secrets.

programme.title: The New Frontier: Atmosphere
programme.desc: How do astronauts cope with living in space?

programme.title: MOON SHINE
programme.desc: The International drive to return to the Moon is gathering pace, this multi- nation effort is readying to return, stay and utilise the Lunar assets.

programme.title: The New Frontier: The Moons of Olympis
programme.desc: These are the the Moon's of the gas giants. Each a unique and mysterious world of its own. Some have oceans of water, geysers of Sulphur or atmospheres of plastic.

programme.title: The New Frontier: Planet X
programme.desc: The New Horizons mission continues deeper into space, it’s next target looming close.

programme.title: The New Frontier: Where Are All the Aliens?
programme.desc: We’re looking hard, but the numbers aren’t stacking up, is life on other planets that hard to find? Even signals from an advanced race elude us.

programme.title: The New Frontier: Gravity
programme.desc: LISA pathfinder, has led the way for a space based gravity wave detector, what are gravity waves and why are they so hard to detect?

programme.title: STAR LIGHT, STAR BRIGHT
programme.desc: The two current science missions to our star; the sun have made great leaps forward in the science of how stars work, how they generate solar winds and how they affect their orbiting planets

programme.title: Wild Assassins: Venom
programme.desc: Welcome to the world of wild assassins, where venomous creatures roam.

programme.title: Wildest Arctic: Arctic Ocean: The Frozen Sea
programme.desc: The film follows a year in the High Arctic, where life is exceptionally entwined with the seasons. Journey to the Arctic where in winter, the sun doesn't rise for months on end, but in the summer thaws out in an explosion of life.

programme.title: Wild Sex

programme.title: Anthony Bourdain Parts Unknown

programme.title: Anthony Bourdain Parts Unknown

programme.title: Anthony Bourdain Parts Unknown

programme.title: Anthony Bourdain Parts Unknown
programme.desc: The two funniest and most brilliant chef/restaurateurs in Canada, Joe Beef's Dave McMillan and Fred Morin, take Tony across Canada by rail.

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter: Match Point
programme.desc: Duty calls and the Chapmans are off on a hunt. The team chases a fugitive who has disappeared into a world of "no known addresses."

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter: Menehune Mischief
programme.desc: When Beth returns to the compound on the Big Island she finds a damaged SUV and two possible culprits - Dog or Justin. Pressed by Beth, Dog offers a third option. The perpetrators are Hawaii's mythical mischief makers - the Menehune!

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter: Mister Mom
programme.desc: It's been quite a while since Tim Chapman put down his mace can and bulletproof vest to care for his growing family. When his old friend Dog calls asking for help tracking an elusive felon, Tim jumps into action.

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter: Bait & Snitch
programme.desc: With the aid of the runner's girlfriend the posse heads out to bring a client with a checked past in. Little does the team suspect that before the end of the day Dog will be helping to mend a family torn apart by violence.

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter: Scared Straight
programme.desc: Dog tracks a fugitive who is on the run from the law for the first time. Reading the file Dog knows the young man's life is at the crossroads. Will he be able to track down this wanna-be-gangsta in time to convince him to make the right choice?

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter: All My Children
programme.desc: For a guy who started life as a free spirited outlaw-biker Dog has certainly turned into a family man. His children touch everything he does today, from searching for the perfect gift for his daughter Cecily's birthday to hunting a fugitive.

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter: Welcome to the Jungle
programme.desc: The hunt for an elusive and clever criminal takes Dog through the world of a bantam fighting cock, a pregnant daughter, and a jungle safe house go together. And don't forget the mopeds as well as a group of hostile squatters on government land!

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter: Bounty Baby
programme.desc: What's a father to do? When the cops ask Dog for help tracking down a renegade teen with a history of running hard, Baby Lyssa, eight months pregnant and counting, insists on joining the hunt. Can Dog keep his daughter safe and catch his man?

programme.title: Duck Dynasty: Governor's Travels
programme.desc: When the Governor of Louisiana announces that he will be coming to Duck Commander to present Willie with a commerce award, John Luke volunteers to give the introduction speech.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty: Quack and Gown

programme.title: Duck Dynasty: Hands on a Woodchipper
programme.desc: When Willie agrees to sponsor Mountain Man's radio contest, a "Hands on a Hardbody" competition, he somehow finds himself competing against Si for Mountain Man's last minute prize: a woodchipper.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty: Brand of Brothers
programme.desc: When Willie contemplates re-branding the Duck Commander image, Jase takes issue with having an outsider design their image, so he and the guys create their own new logo and business cards. Meanwhile, Willie and Phil help Bella with her science project.

programme.title: Embedded 45' - Shooting War in Germany: Embedded 45 - Shooting War in Germany 2

programme.title: Combat Ships
programme.desc: Explore the design flaws and human errors that condemned great ships to a tragic end. In 1628 the warship Vasa sank on her maiden voyage. Together with Vasa curator Fred Hocker, solve the mystery of why this great ship sank.

programme.title: Combat Ships

programme.desc: Lucy & Martin travel to Folkestone to see a Victorian house that could offer someone a foot on the property ladder. Then it's on to Hertfordshire to see a 3 bed terrace and in Devon there's an old inn up for sale where last orders have been called.

programme.title: Arthur: Arthur Goes To Camp/Buster Makes The Grade
programme.desc: Arthur and his friends go to summer camp where it's strictly boys vs. girls. Next, Buster Baxter is everyone's favorite jokester. Unfortunately, though, Buster's grades aren't as good as his jokes, and Buster may have to repeat third grade.

programme.title: Arthur: The Great MacGrady, Parts 1 & 2
programme.desc: When Mrs. MacGrady is diagnosed with cancer, Arthur and his friends all react differently to the news. The kids must learn how to come together to help support each other and Mrs. MacGrady.

programme.title: Arthur: Arthur's Treasure Hunt/The Return Of The King
programme.desc: When Buster digs up an ancient arrowhead, everyone else wants to hunt for buried treasures, too. And a field trip to the Medieval Fair pits Arthur's class against another led by Ratburn's super-tough mentor, Mr. Pryce-Jones.

programme.title: Arthur: What Scared Sue Ellen?/Clarissa Is Cracked
programme.desc: Sue Ellen's not afraid of much, but when she encounters a really creepy howling noise in the woods, she's scared for real. Then, Grandma Thora has a lot of fun stuff, especially her porcelain doll, Clarissa.

programme.title: Arthur: Arthur And The Big Riddle/Double Dare
programme.desc: Alex Trebek lends his voice as gameshow host Alex Lebek in this episode which poses the age-old riddle--is winning really everything? What if your friends dared you to skip homework--and then double dared you to skip school?

programme.title: Arthur
programme.desc: no info available

programme.title: Arthur: Arthur Weighs In/The Law of the Jungle Gym
programme.desc: When Arthur doesn't fit into his costume for the play, he's shocked to learn that he's gotten "husky-sized." He gets serious about getting in shape. Muffy found the perfect place to take pictures with her new digital camera: the jungle gym.

programme.title: Arthur: Arthur Writes A Story/Arthur's Lost Dog
programme.desc: Struggling with a school assignment, Arthur decides that his regular life is just too boring to write about. And, Arthur is the only one who believes in the superior intelligence of his dog. But even Arthur gets confused when Pal runs away.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Riffs Musical Zoo - Boats
programme.desc: Everyone is excited about an upcoming trip to the zoo. When the trip is postponed, Riff decides to create a musical zoo. He gathers instruments that sound like real animals. Everyone has a wonderful time at Riff's musical zoo.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: A game of hide and seek takes a turn when Baby Bop claims that an elephant is hiding in the park!/BJ and Riff engage in a friendly contest to see which of them can find the loudest sound in the park.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Sweeter Than Candy
programme.desc: Barney, Baby Bop and BJ are helping the park kids get ready for a school play. When Barney tells everyone that putting on shows began in Greece, they want to go visit! While there, they meet some friends and decide to put on a play in an amphitheatre.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: China - Welcome, Cousin Riff
programme.desc: BJ is waiting for a very special letter from his pen pal in China. The letter reveals that his pen pal is coming for a visit. With the help of friends in the park, BJ hurries to get the park ready.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Three Lines, Three Corners
programme.desc: Barney and his friends play hopscotch—with a twist. Whitney is learning about triangles at school, so Angela created Triangle Hopscotch to help reinforce "triangles."

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Making a Move
programme.desc: Welcome to the neighborhood! Anna has just moved to the neighborhood and is missing her old friends. Barney and his friends help her discover that moving is an adventure with new places to explore and new friends to meet.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Home Sweet Earth
programme.desc: It's Earth Day, a great time for everyone to learn how to help take car of our world, but Ben is wasting paper while BJ isn't too concerned about cutting down a tree to make room for a new playground.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Imagine That!
programme.desc: While Baby Bop and BJ are pretending to be pirates, Barney creates a magical island for their fantastic adventure. Together they discover ways to have fun while playing dress up, pretending, and being silly.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Turtles at the Earth's Core
programme.desc: The Turtles venture down to the Earth's core and discover a lost prehistoric world complete with dinosaurs.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): April Foolish
programme.desc: Emperor Aleister and his daughter Princess Mallory arrive from the small kingdom of Malacuria where there has been a recent discovery of a vast deposit of Lydium - 90, the most powerful source of energy found on Earth.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Attack of the Big MACC
programme.desc: At an army testing lab where a super laser is being demonstrated, the crowd is surprised by the arrival of MACC, a robot from the year 2399 that talks like a cowboy from watching old western movies on inter-dimensional trips.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): The Ninja Sword of Nowhere
programme.desc: Krang detects the existence of an alien metal that could help him conquer the Earth. It happens to be on a Ninja Sword the Turtles have purchased for Splinter as a National Sensei Appreciation Day gift.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): 20,000 Leaks Under the City
programme.desc: Krang is out to liquidate the Turtles by pumping water through the Earth's crust and into the sewer system. The super pump starts sucking up the entire ocean and soon the streets are covered with three feet of water.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Take Me to Your Leader
programme.desc: Leonardo cannot take the pressure of being the leader of the Turtles anymore and leaves. Meanwhile, Krang and Shredder use their Solar Siphon to drain all the energy from the Sun.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): The Four Musketurtles
programme.desc: Leonardo becomes a little soft in the shell after he is knocked on the head. He thinks he is D' Artagan and that the other Turtles are the Three Musketeers.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Turtles, Turtles, Everywhere!
programme.desc: The city's new state-of-the-art garbage disposal system's main computer DUMP 1000 is programmed by Shredder to seize the Turtles but starts scooping up every turtle in sight.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Cowabunga Shredhead
programme.desc: With the aid of the Holographic Synthesizer, Shredder creates an exact likeness of Michelangelo and programs it to invade the Turtles' lair and destroy them.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Invasion of the Turtle Snatchers
programme.desc: Donatello and Rocksteady are beamed up to a space ship - by little alien, Nikto, six thousand years old, traveling with his parents Klaatu and Barada.

programme.title: Pokemon: Indigo League
programme.desc: On his way to the next Gym Battle challenge, Ash, and his friends arrive at a massive mansion that seems to be a hidden ninjas' lair, appearing lost. Could this be the Fuchsia Gym?

programme.title: Pokemon: Indigo League
programme.desc: In the place of the injured Lara, can Ash handle her fiery Ponyta and guide it to win the annual race?

programme.title: Pokemon: Indigo League
programme.desc: Our heroes accompany Officer Jenny into the forest, intent on pursuing some poachers. Soon, they discover a young boy amidst a pack of Kangaskhan. His name is Tommy, and no one knows the secret of his childhood…

programme.title: Pokemon: Indigo League
programme.desc: After they encounter a very long bridge that can be only crossed by bike, a job delivering medicine is just the ticket for Ash and friends to reach Sunnytown!

programme.title: Pokemon: Indigo League
programme.desc: Caught in a sudden rain shower, our heroes quickly try to seek some shelter. Dragging a very reluctant Misty, Ash, and Brock go inside a cabin, and instantly get the impression that they are not alone!

programme.title: Pokemon: Indigo League
programme.desc: The gang discovers a large group of wild Pikachu, and though they first seem frightened by the intruder, the group eventually opens up to Ash’s Pikachu. How will Pikachu feel seeing others of its kind in the wild?

programme.title: Pokemon: Indigo League
programme.desc: Ash and his friends find a large home with a lavish garden party in full swing, attended by the Trainers of Pokémon who evolve using Evolution stones. Maybe they can see the seemingly abandoned Eevee’s Trainer here?

programme.title: Pokemon: Indigo League
programme.desc: Our hungry heroes arrive at a small village. In return for a meal, Ash, Misty, and Brock agree to investigate what is causing the river to dry up, destroying the village’s harvest.

programme.title: Pokemon: Indigo League
programme.desc: We join our heroes on the outskirts of Dark City, which appears to be deserted. Even during the middle of the day, there is no one on the streets. What has happened to make this place so desolate?

programme.title: Pokemon: Indigo League
programme.desc: Ash is hard at work, continuing his rigorous training schedule even over spring vacation—until our heroes discover there’s a traveling carnival nearby! Ash, Misty, and Brock quickly join in the fun of the fair!

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Dipsy is out for a walk when a strong wind blows his hat away. The hat disappears into Home Hill, but Dipsy doesn't know where it has gone. Noo Noo blows the hat back out of Home Hill to Dipsy, who is very happy.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Noo-noo wakes up the Teletubbies to watch some children paint a huge mural using spray paints. Laa-Laa is making Tubby Custard, but the Tubby Custard machine makes a big Tubby Custard squirt on Tinky Winky, Dipsy, Po and Laa-Laa.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Laa-Laa and Dipsy hug and run off to watch some children making some fancy dress clothes. A crown appears in Teletubbyland and when Tinky Winky wears the crown he becomes King Tinky Winky.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: A voice trumpet rises to announce the colour purple. Some children explore the colours purple. In Teletubbyland a big beautiful purple flower grows.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies watch some children play with balls of different shapes and sizes. In Teletubbyland, something happens to Dipsy's hat.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: A voice trumpet brings a message that makes Laa-Laa laugh. The Teletubbies watch some children learning about the number one. The Teletubbies take turns to wear the skirt.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies watch some children learn about number eight. Eight clouds appear in Teletubbyland and the Teletubbies learn count to eight. The animals march through Teletubbyland.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies watch some children exploring the colour red. Po makes some adjustments on the switch panel and all the lights change to the colour red. Po sees some red clouds and changes the colour of all the flowers to red.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Laa-Laa dances with her ball. A little boy tells us why he likes to live in his block of flats. A balloon appears in Teletubbyland. The Teletubbies follow a balloon.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: A voice trumpet rises to teach the Teletubbies the rhyme ‘How Now Brown Cow’. The Teletubbies watch some children explore the colour brown.

programme.title: Mission: Impossible: Flip Side
programme.desc: Sal Mineo guest stars as a ruthless young operator who heads up an international narcotics ring which the IMF attempts to break up on Paramount Television's "Mission: Impossible."

programme.title: Mission: Impossible: My Friend, My Enemy
programme.desc: Special-guest star Jill Haworth portrays a glamorous double agent, who uses her feminine wiles on Paris to set him up for an enemy plot on Paramount Television's "Mission: Impossible" series.

programme.title: Mission: Impossible: The Innocent
programme.desc: Christopher Connelly guest stars as a non-cooperative young computer genius who has "dropped out" of society, but is desperately needed by the IMF to complete their assignment on Paramount Television's "Mission: Impossible."

programme.title: Mission: Impossible: Decoy
programme.desc: Phelps falls in love with a woman that the IMF has been assigned to bring out of an iron curtain country over the seemingly insurmountable obstacles set in their way by the country's secret police in the "Decoy"

programme.title: Mission: Impossible: Flight
programme.desc: John Colicos guest stars as a rebellious Caribbean government official, who plans to take over his country by having his president assassinated while in the United States to speak before a joint session of Congress

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Gangster Whiskey
programme.desc: Allen and Ton trek to an auction in Moreno Valley, CA, where two locker wins yield a vintage jukebox, a valuable baseball card and a Prohibition-era moonshine still.

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Whip It Good
programme.desc: With a motocross bike and a rare stock whip up for grabs, Ton and Allen face one of their toughest competitors yet: their former employee Carolyn, who knows all their tricks, has money to burn and is out for revenge.

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Ton's Got a Gun
programme.desc: In the affluent area of Mission Hills, CA, Allen and Ton pick up an estate unit that contains a minibike, a Depression-era slot machine and an 1871 Colt revolver.

programme.title: Auction Hunters: The Fall Guys
programme.desc: The pressures of running a shop are taking a toll on Ton and Allen and not even the discovery of a stunt-man's fall bag can ease the tension. When things get heated, Ton abandons the partnership and leaves town.

programme.title: Auction Hunters: You Foos You Lose
programme.desc: When their truck breaks down, Ton and Allen lay it all on the line to raise money to fix her. They score a Foosball table, Tommy Gun drums and to Ton's delight, a treasure trove of Twinkies, but will it be enough?

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Haff-Inated
programme.desc: Allen's off his game without his morning coffee, but some fun items help him re-energize. The guys find a wrestling ring, and when Allen uncovers vintage animatronics, they remind him of his days as a theme park boat captain.

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Battle Bought
programme.desc: Mint 1800s French "Harmonica Pistol" and a collection of '90s remote controlled combat robots.

programme.title: Auction Hunters: The Unsinkable Ton
programme.desc: Unique finds lead the guys to some cool sales. They try to authenticate props from the film Titanic, Ton faces off with a rattler during a snakebite kit sale, and Allen gets into ice carving while unloading some chainsaws.

programme.title: Auction Hunters: The Jersey Score
programme.desc: Ton & Allen attend a silent auction in NJ, but that doesn't stop the local buyers from letting them know they're not welcome! They uncover a skeet throwing machine from the 1960's & a vintage vehicle with some real horsepower!

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Miami Heat
programme.desc: All-terrain Air Commander Hovercraft, a 1970s NASA countdown clock and a one-of-a-kind 1960s Delta Relay rocket.

programme.title: Teen Mom: Too Much Too Soon
programme.desc: Maci confronts Kyle when she sees her new life in Nashville isn't what she expected. Amber breaks up with Gary and starts dating. Farrah gets the results of Sophia's DNA test.

programme.title: Teen Mom: See You Later
programme.desc: Catelynn and Tyler have an emotional reunion with their daughter. Amber and Gary try to work out custody of Leah now that they are broken up, and Farrah's mum sets her up on a date.

programme.title: Teen Mom: Catch Up Special
programme.desc: Special episode in front of a studio audience. Maci, Farrah, Amber and Catelynn discuss their ups and downs, achievements and regrets. Last in the series.

programme.title: Teen Mom: Unseen Moments
programme.desc: Dr. Drew hosts an exclusive look at never-before seen laughs, fights and other Season 2 drama with Farrah, Maci, Catelynn, and Amber.

programme.title: Teen Mom: Baby Talk
programme.desc: Maci, Catelynn, Amber and Farrah host a look back at the cutest, most funny, and most difficult baby moments of series 2. They will also discuss their experience as young mothers.

programme.title: The Maury Show: I'll Prove You Are Cheating on My Teen!
programme.desc: Instead of chocolates and dinners, these guests are being cheated on and dumped. Love may be blind, but lie detector tests and hidden cameras will reveal the truth!

programme.title: The Maury Show: I'm Back for My 3rd Test... I'll Prove I'm Not the Father Again
programme.desc: Chris cheated and impregnated Lakin's neighbor. He denies he fathered Lakin’s baby. Then, John’s back for a 3rd DNA test. Can Jade prove that he’s the father or is her record 0-3?

programme.title: The Maury Show: Teen DNA... I'll Prove You Got Me Pregnant at 15!
programme.desc: Keith is terrified that Chyna cheated and their daughter may not be his. Could Keith’s best friend be the real father? Will the DNA results quash Omar's denials about fathering Jennifer’s child?

programme.title: The Maury Show: Is My Man Sleeping With My Teen Nephew?
programme.desc: Nikki won’t marry Will until he proves he’s faithful, but what did our hidden cameras confirm? Then, teens Jessica and Sean are days away from marriage. Could Sean be cheating with her best friend?

programme.title: The Maury Show: I Slept With Your Fiance... I'll Prove My 2 Kids Are His!
programme.desc: Did Selina's fiancé Thomas secretly father a baby with his ex-girlfriend and has Victoria been cheating on Brian? They'll take the test to find out on Maury today!

programme.title: The Maury Show: 5 Men Tested… Are Any of Them My Baby's Dad?
programme.desc: Tony cheated on his wife Tiff and impregnated another woman. Now he’s denying both of their kids! Plus, more shocking tales of mad moms, denying dads and DNA drama!

programme.title: Avatar: The Last Airbender: Ep 6
programme.desc: Aang and Zuko are taken on parallel adventures that give them insight into their forefathers' pasts, but how does the tale of Roku and Sozin matter to them now?

programme.title: Avatar: The Last Airbender: Ep 7
programme.desc: When Toph discovers a quick way to make cash, Katara disapproves, and the rift between them has disastrous consequences.

programme.title: Avatar: The Last Airbender: Ep 8
programme.desc: In a spooky village, Katara makes a special connection; the group investigate strange disappearances that occur during full moons.

programme.title: Avatar: The Last Airbender: Ep 9

programme.title: Avatar: The Last Airbender: Ep 10

programme.title: Avatar: The Last Airbender: Ep 11
programme.desc: As the invasion force fights their way to the Fire Nation capital, the kids help Aang find the Firelord in time for the eclipse, but after a few surprises.

programme.title: Avatar: The Last Airbender: Ep 12
programme.desc: When the group seek refuge at the Western Air temple, they find someone they weren't expecting has been following them and hopes to join their group.

programme.title: Avatar: The Last Airbender: Ep 13

programme.title: Avatar: The Last Airbender: Ep 14
programme.desc: Sokka and Zuko head to the Fire Nation's most heavily guarded prison - the Boiling Rock - in hopes to find and break-out the captured invasion force.

programme.title: Avatar: The Last Airbender: Ep 15

programme.title: Weird Or What? With William Shatner: Man Beasts

programme.title: Weird Or What? With William Shatner: Weird Animal Behaviour
programme.desc: Weird Animal Behaviour: Three extraordinary stories about the supersensory power of animals, including a psychic octopus that correctly predicted multiple World Cup winners.

programme.title: Weird Or What? With William Shatner: Ghosts In The Machine

programme.title: Weird Or What? With William Shatner: Premonitions
